reasoning: A and D both nail the structural insight (negative skew makes 19/20 the expected signature, not evidence of edge) and add genuine bias work: A names the substitution and resulting/anchoring traps and grounds it with a binomial intuition (5% per-quarter tail makes a clean 20q run unremarkable) plus convexity, a premortem, and a Ulysses contract; D adds the turkey problem, survivorship (the blown-up desks not in the room), and explicit incentive/fragility framing. A edges D on tighter statistical calibration and a more complete safeguard set. C is excellent and uniquely flags illiquid-OTM mismarking and the desk-to-firm decision-owner shift, but does slightly less explicit cognitive-trap and base-rate work. B is competent (ES-not-VaR, loss-to-premium math, model validation) but reads as a checklist with the least depth, no premortem/survivorship/non-linearity treatment and weak bias surfacing.
